info.title: Vertifile API
info.description: Document protection and verification platform. Vertifile creates tamper-proof PVF (Protected Verification File) documents with HMAC-SHA256 signatures, blind hashing, and optional blockchain anchoring on Polygon.

tags.name: Gateway
tags.description: Server-side PVF intake and batch verification

tags.name: well-known
tags.description: Public well-known endpoints for transparency and auditing

tags.name: user
tags.description: User dashboard — documents, profile, stamp config

tags.name: onboarding
tags.description: Onboarding wizard — verification codes, state management

post.summary: Sign up for an API key
post.description: Register a new organization and receive an API key. Pro plan allows 100 requests per day. Limited to 3 signups per IP per day.

Upload a document and receive a tamper-proof PVF file. The server blind-hashes the content (never reads it), signs with HMAC-SHA256, and returns an obfuscated HTML file with live verification, screen capture detection, and DevTools detection. Rate limited to 50 requests per hour.

schema.description: The .pvf file (obfuscated HTML with embedded verification)

post.description: Public endpoint. Verify a document by its SHA-256 hash. Optionally include the HMAC signature, recipient hash, and code integrity token for full verification. Returns a fresh session token on success. Rate limited to 500 requests per 15 minutes.

get.description: Trust-minimized Ed25519 signature verification. Anyone can call this endpoint (no auth, CORS open) to verify a Vertifile document's signature using the public key Vertifile publishes at /.well-known/vertifile-pubkey.pem. This endpoint does NOT touch the documents table — it only verifies the cryptographic math against the published public key. Rate limited to 500 requests per 15 minutes per IP.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the document (64 chars)

parameters.name: signature
parameters.description: Ed25519 signature over the canonical payload, base64url-encoded (86 chars, no padding)

parameters.name: keyId
parameters.description: 16 lowercase hex chars identifying the signing key

parameters.name: payload
parameters.description: The canonical pipe-separated payload that was signed: hash|orgId|createdAt|recipientHash|codeIntegrity

200.description: Verification result. Note: always returns 200 even for invalid signatures — check the 'valid' field.

fingerprint.description: Full SHA-256 hex of the canonical public key PEM (only present on valid:true)

post.description: Heartbeat endpoint called every 30 seconds by PVF viewers to refresh the session token. If the token was created less than 4 minutes ago, the cached token is returned.

post.summary: Verify and extract a PVF file
post.description: Upload a .pvf file for server-side verification. Extracts the hash and signature, verifies against the database, and returns the original embedded document as base64. Fires webhooks on successful verification.
Upload and verify multiple .pvf files in a single request. Each file is independently verified.

document.description: Document metadata (only present when verified is true)

get.description: Returns usage statistics for the authenticated organization, including document counts and verification metrics.
Returns a paginated list of documents created by the authenticated organization.

post.description: Register a webhook URL to receive verification event notifications. The URL must be HTTPS and publicly accessible. A signing secret is returned for payload verification via the X-Vertifile-Signature header.

get.description: Returns all webhooks registered for the authenticated organization.

delete.description: Remove a registered webhook by its ID. The webhook must belong to the authenticated organization.

get.description: Lightweight health check returning service status, version, and uptime.
Detailed health check including database statistics (document and organization counts) and blockchain connection status. Requires admin authentication.
Returns a JSON description of all API endpoints, authentication methods, and SDK usage.

schema.description: Full API documentation including endpoints, security info, and SDK examples

post.description: Operator-triggered cache clear for the Ed25519 active primary key. Used during key rotation to force immediate pickup of the new active key.

activeKeyId.description: The new active key ID after cache invalidation, or null if no active key

get.description: Returns Prometheus-format metrics for heap usage, RSS, uptime, and PostgreSQL connection pool state. Operator-only.
Returns operational status of all critical service URIs, database, blockchain, and Ed25519 signing. Public endpoint, no auth required. Rate limited to 30 requests per minute.

post.description: Send a contact form message. Sends a notification to the operator and a confirmation to the submitter. Rate limited to 3 requests per hour per IP.

post.summary: Create a demo PVF (no auth required)
post.description: Public demo endpoint. Upload a document and receive a .pvf file without an API key. Rate limited to 5 requests per hour per IP. Responds with the binary PVF file by default, or JSON when the Accept header is application/json or ?format=json is set.

file.description: Document to protect (PDF, image, text). Max 50 MB.

200.description: PVF created. Default response is the binary .pvf file. Pass ?format=json or Accept: application/json for the JSON shape below.

get.description: Returns the authenticated organization's profile including plan, document count, rate limit, and branding configuration.
Returns the organization's branding settings: custom icon, brand color, and wave color.

post.description: Update branding settings for the authenticated organization. Colors must be hex (#RRGGBB). Custom icon must be SVG or a data URI.

customIcon.description: SVG string or data:image/... URI. Max ~512 KB.

post.description: Create a new email+password account. Password must be at least 8 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character. Returns the new user and starts an authenticated session.

password.description: Must contain uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character

name.description: Display name (optional; defaults to email local part)

post.summary: Log in with email and password
post.description: Authenticate with email and password. Account locks for 15 minutes after 5 consecutive failures. Sets a session cookie on success.
Destroys the current session and clears the session cookie.

get.description: Returns the full profile of the currently authenticated user, including plan, document usage, and onboarding status.

post.description: Sends a password reset link to the given email if an account exists. Always returns the same response to prevent email enumeration. Rate limited to 3 requests per email per hour.

200.description: Reset email dispatched (or silently suppressed if email not found)

message.example: If this email exists, a reset link has been sent

post.description: Validates the reset token (valid for 30 minutes) and updates the user password. Invalidates all existing sessions for the user.

token.description: Reset token from the emailed link (64 hex chars)

password.description: New password (same complexity rules as registration)

message.example: Password updated successfully. Please sign in with your new password.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile as used by the dashboard, including admin flag, document quota, and stamp config.
Returns the user's current visual stamp configuration used on PVF documents.

post.description: Saves the user's stamp visual configuration. The config object is free-form JSON (max 10 KB). Updates the stamp cache immediately.

schema.description: Stamp configuration object. Shape is client-defined; the server stores it as-is.

get.description: Returns a paginated, filterable list of documents belonging to the authenticated user. The Ed25519 raw signature blob is stripped; a boolean is_dual_signed field is returned instead.

parameters.name: starred
parameters.description: Return only starred documents when true

post.description: Upload a document from the user dashboard. Accepts PDF, PNG, JPEG, or plain text (max 50 MB). Creates a PVF and returns a share URL. Paid-plan users receive a download link; free/trial users receive a preview-only response.

file.description: Document to protect. Allowed: PDF, PNG, JPEG, plain text. Max 50 MB.

200.description: Document protected. Paid plan returns hash+shareId; free/trial returns preview-only shape.

overage.description: True if user exceeded their document quota

preview.description: Present and true for free/trial accounts

previewUrl.description: Viewer URL (present when preview is true)

post.description: Zero-knowledge PVF 2.0 upload. The client encrypts the document with AES-256-GCM before upload; the server only receives the encrypted blob, client-computed SHA-256 hash, and IV. The AES key never touches the server. Idempotent — replays of the same hash return the existing shareId.

hash.description: Client-computed SHA-256 hex of original content

mimeType.description: Original MIME type of the unencrypted document

deduplicated.description: True when the same document was already uploaded by this user

preview.description: Present and true for free/trial accounts

post.description: Toggle the starred state on a document owned by the authenticated user.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: SHA-256 hex hash of the document (64 chars)

starred.description: True to star, false to unstar

delete.description: Permanently deletes a document owned by the authenticated user and removes its PVF file from storage.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: SHA-256 hex hash of the document (64 chars)

put.description: Update the display name of the authenticated user. Name is HTML-escaped and capped at 100 characters.

post.description: Change the password for an email-based account. Requires the current password. Invalidates all other active sessions, preserving the current one.

delete.description: Permanently delete the authenticated user's account and all associated data.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's branding settings (custom icon, brand color, wave color).

post.description: Save branding settings. brandColor must be hex (#RRGGBB). waveColor can be a single hex or a JSON array of 3-10 hex colors. customIcon must be an SVG string or a data:image/ URI (max ~512 KB).

get.description: Returns the API key linked to the authenticated user's organization. Returns null if no key has been generated yet.

apiKey.description: API key (vf_live_... prefix) or null if not yet generated

post.description: Generates a new API key for the authenticated user's organization (vf_live_ prefix, 48 hex chars). Idempotent — returns the existing key if one already exists.
Generates a 6-digit verification code and sends it to the given email address. Rate limited to 3 sends per email per hour and 20 requests per 15 minutes globally.

200.description: Code sent (or queued). In development environments the response also includes _dev_code.

post.description: Validates the 6-digit code sent to the user's email. Marks the user's email as verified on success. Maximum 5 attempts; code expires after 10 minutes. Rate limited to 10 requests per 15 minutes.

attempts_remaining.description: Remaining attempts before code is locked

get.description: Returns the current wizard step, selections, and stamp configuration for the authenticated user. Returns default values if the wizard has not yet been started.

selections.description: Wizard answer map keyed by question id

stamp_config.description: Partial stamp config set during the wizard

put.description: Upsert wizard progress. All three body fields are optional but at least one must be present. Object fields (selections, stamp_config) are merged with the existing stored value using JSONB merge.

selections.description: Partial wizard answer map to merge

post.description: Finalizes the onboarding wizard. Reads the stored state from the database, persists user profile fields and stamp configuration, marks the user account as onboarding-complete, and redirects to /app.

get.description: Returns the active Ed25519 signing public key in PEM format. Cached for 1 hour. CORS open. Use this to verify PVF documents offline without calling /api/verify.
Returns the set of active Ed25519 public keys in JWKS format. CORS open. Cached for 1 hour.

items.description: JWK entry for an Ed25519 public key

get.description: Machine-readable security policy disclosure as defined by RFC 9116. Served from the static file at public/.well-known/security.txt. Cached for 24 hours.
Read-only endpoint exposing key rotation history for transparency and third-party auditors. No authentication required.

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: API key obtained from POST /api/signup. Prefix: vf_live_

AdminSecretAuth.name: X-Admin-Secret
AdminSecretAuth.description: Operator-only admin secret for privileged administrative actions

SessionCookieAuth.name: connect.sid
SessionCookieAuth.description: Session cookie set by /auth/login or /auth/register. Required for all /auth/* and /api/user/* endpoints.

message.example: Save this API key — it will not be shown again.

file.description: Document file to protect (PDF, image, or text). Max 50 MB.

recipient.description: Optional recipient email for binding. The PVF will be locked to this recipient.

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the document content

content.description: Legacy: content object (hash will be computed server-side)

recipientHash.description: SHA-256 hash of the recipient email for binding verification

codeIntegrity.description: Code integrity token from the PVF viewer

hash.description: The hash that was looked up

reason.description: Failure reason (only present when verified is false)

token.description: Fresh session token (only present when verified is true)

blockchain.description: Blockchain proof (if on-chain mode is active)

hash.description: Document hash for the active PVF session

expiresIn.description: Token expiry in seconds (30 for fresh, 300 for cached)

cached.description: Whether a cached token was returned (present only when true)

file.description: A .pvf file to verify and extract

reason.description: Failure reason (only present when verified is false)

document.description: Original document metadata (only present when verified is true)

reason.description: Failure reason (only present when verified is false)

document.description: Document metadata (only present when verified is true)

total.description: Total document count for the organization

url.description: HTTPS webhook endpoint URL (must be publicly accessible, no private/internal IPs)

secret.description: HMAC signing secret for verifying webhook payloads via X-Vertifile-Signature header

AuthUser.description: User object returned by login and register endpoints

UserDocument.description: Document record as returned by the user dashboard list endpoint

is_dual_signed.description: True when the document carries an Ed25519 signature in addition to HMAC

RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum requests allowed in the window

RateLimit-Remaining.description: Remaining requests in the current window

RateLimit-Reset.description: Seconds until the rate limit resets

example.error: Too many requests, try again later
